exemplar.title: A Comparison of Theo and Humbert as Unreliable Narrators in The Silent Patient and Lolita

detailedExemplarData.title: A Comparison of Theo and Humbert as Unreliable Narrators in The Silent Patient and Lolita

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: A clear, descriptive title and consistent section headings provide strong focus and guide the readerâs expectations.
The research question is explicitly stated and generally maintained throughout the analysis, demonstrating sustained inquiry.
weaknesses: Methodology description lacks detail on source selection criteria and analytical procedures, limiting methodological clarity.
Occasional digressions (e.g. extended Freudian theory discussion) slightly weaken the precision of focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates accurate and effective communication of the topic and a focused research question, but the methodology strand remains only adequately described. Two high-band performances (topic clarity and question focus) are balanced by a mid-band methodology (3/6), yielding an overall mid-band result. A score of 4/6 reflects strong clarity and focus tempered by gaps in methodological rigor.

strengths: Good integration of primary and secondary sources shows coherent understanding of plot, characterisation and key themes.
Use of psychoanalytic and sociological concepts generally conveys accurate meaning and supports analysis.
weaknesses: Terminology is at times imprecise (e.g. âthe second booksâ) and occasional phrasing disrupts the academic register.
Citation details are inconsistent (missing publication years, mixed styles), undermining source credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates solid knowledge of the topic and acceptable use of subject-specific concepts, but inconsistencies in terminology and citation prevent fully consistent application. Averaging a sub-score of 4/6 in knowledge and 3/6 in terminology yields an overall 3/6 for this criterion.

strengths: Insightful close readings (e.g. Nabokovâs imagery, Michaelidesâs rhetorical devices) underpin a focused argument on power dynamics.
Effective synthesis of empirical findings (age-gap studies) and trauma parallels deepens the comparative analysis.
Clear recognition of narrative reliability layers (diary vs. therapist account) shows critical awareness.
weaknesses: Evaluation is uneven: some claims (e.g. societal attitudes) are asserted without sufficient critical interrogation.
Several passages revert to plot summary or extended quotation without adequate analytical commentary.
Alternative interpretations are acknowledged superficially rather than fully explored.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strandsâresearch relevance, analysis depth, and argument evaluationâconsistently fall in the 7â9 band. The work presents good, mostly supported analysis and a coherent, partially critical argument. A score of 7/12 reflects this solid but uneven critical thinking performance.

strengths: The structure is clearly signposted with a comprehensive table of contents and distinct section headings.
Required elements (title page, word count, bibliography, page numbering) are present, aiding navigation.
weaknesses: Formatting inconsistencies (visible HTML tags, erratic line spacing) distract and interrupt reading.
Bibliography entries switch between citation styles and punctuation, undermining overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the essayâs overall structure meets IB requirements (placing it in the 3â4 range), pervasive formatting lapses drop the performance to the lower band. A score of 2/4 acknowledges structural clarity but penalises frequent presentation errors.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The essay outlines a methodology (comparative literary analysis with reference to psychological and sociological studies) and identifies primary and secondary sources.  
â¢  Source range is adequateâcritical articles, theoretical works and primary texts are citedâshowing some informed selection.  
â¢  However, the rationale for choosing particular theories, the criteria for source reliability, and any explanation of research procedures are only briefly sketched, leading to gaps in methodological clarity. This places the work securely in the 3â4 band; a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

Basic layout requirements (word count, title, page numbering) are satisfied, and the document is broadly readable.  
â¢  However, formatting inconsistencies are frequent: visible HTML/markdown tags remain, bibliography entries switch between citation styles, and line spacing/punctuation are erratic. These lapses indicate only partial adherence to IB formatting conventions.  
â¢  Consequently, the strand sits in the 1â2 band, with a score of 1 reflecting performance at the lower end.

The bibliography lists a reasonable breadth of literary criticism, psychological studies and classical sources, indicating **good but not exhaustive research**.  
â¢  Many sources are cited in-text; however, citation style is inconsistent and some sweeping claims (e.g., prevalence of male attraction to children) are insufficiently sourced or critically interrogated, reducing evidential rigour.  
â¢  The overall relevance to the research question is mostly clear, yet select sources are mentioned only briefly. This meets the lower end of the 4-6 band, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

The studentâs topic is announced explicitly in the title, reiterated in the opening paragraph and consistently sign-posted through clear section headings and a detailed table of contents.  
â¢  The introduction supplies sufficient background on the concept of the unreliable narrator, immediately linking it to the chosen primary texts and to the wider notion of taboo relationships.  
â¢  Although occasional digressions occur later in the essay, the overall communication of purpose remains strong and unambiguous, demonstrating a clear focus appropriate to the upper band, hence a score of 5 within the 5â6 range.

The discussion is organised around clear thematic sections (methodology, individual analyses, comparative analysis, taboo relationships, conclusion), and an argument about how unreliability illuminates power dynamics is discernible.  
â¢  Nevertheless, evaluation of evidence is uneven: alternative readings are acknowledged only in passing, and some conclusions (e.g., societal acceptance of paedophilia) are asserted without nuanced critique, producing partial rather than fully critical evaluation.  
â¢  Overall this reflects competent but not consistently rigorous critical thinking, meriting a score of 4 within the 4-6 band.

The essay offers numerous close readingsâe.g., analysis of Nabokovâs opening lines and Michaelidesâs rhetorical questionsâthat move beyond description to interpret issues of power, sympathy and manipulation.  
â¢  Links between textual features and overarching argument are usually clear, though some paragraphs slide into extended plot summary or rely on assertion rather than analytical demonstration, limiting depth.  
â¢  Given the generally insightful but occasionally uneven engagement, the work fulfils the mid-band descriptor with some stronger moments; a score of 5/12 within the 4-6 band is justified.

The essay contains all required structural elements: title page with word count, table of contents, introduction, body sections, conclusion and bibliography, enabling clear navigation.  
â¢  Section headings and internal signposting generally support coherence.  
â¢  Minor issuesâsuch as occasional misplaced HTML tags and inconsistent spacingâundermine polish but do not compromise overall structural appropriateness, positioning the work in the 3â4 band; a score of 3 is awarded.

Frequent quotation and summary of both primary novels demonstrate **good knowledge** of plot, characterisation and key themes, and the bibliography shows engagement with relevant secondary literature on unreliable narration, psychology and taboo.  
â¢  The student normally integrates textual evidence effectively, but some contextual claims about male attraction to children or societal attitudes lack firm scholarly backing, indicating occasional over-reliance on assertion.  
â¢  Overall understanding is coherent and more than adequate, though not consistently sophisticated; a score of 4 within the 3â4 band is merited.

Subject terminology such as âunreliable narrator,â âpower dynamics,â ânarrative manipulation,â and psychoanalytic concepts (e.g., Oedipal complex) are used in ways that generally convey accurate meaning.  
â¢  Nonetheless, there are moments of imprecision (e.g., confusing references to âthe second books,â occasional awkward phrasing, and minor mislabelling of criminal acts) that disrupt consistency and academic register.  
â¢  The performance aligns with the mid-band descriptorâmostly accurate, though not fully consistentâwarranting a score of 3 out of 6 in this strand.

The research question is stated on the title page and repeated verbatim in the introduction, framed around how Nabokov and Michaelides employ unreliable narration to explore power dynamics in taboo relationships.  
â¢  Throughout the body of the essay the student generally returns to this question when analysing narrative techniques and their thematic implications, indicating a sustained line of inquiry.  
â¢  Some sections (e.g. extended discussions of Freudian theory) wander slightly from the core focus, preventing full precision, but the linkage between RQ and discussion remains largely intact; this supports a high band, awarded 5/6.

annotations.comment: The student has provided a clear, descriptive title that effectively frames the comparative analysis of unreliable narrators, establishing focus and guiding the readerâs expectations.
The research question is succinctly stated on the title page, clearly articulating the investigationâs scope and anchoring the subsequent analysis.
The opening of the Taboo Relationships section reiterates prior analysis; consider introducing fresh insights or condensing repetitive commentary to maintain analytical momentum.
Effective integration of empirical findings on age gaps enriches the literary argument by grounding it in social research.
In-text quotations lack page numbers for APA-style references; include precise page citations to meet academic conventions.
Citations for Zerweck are inconsistent (missing publication year); adopt a consistent citation style (e.g. APA) and include full bibliographic details to strengthen source credibility.
Inclusion of the word count at the outset meets IB formatting requirements, demonstrating attention to formal submission guidelines.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-organised, enabling efficient navigation through the essayâs major sections.
Visible HTML tags in the table of contents suggest leftover formatting markup; please remove code elements to improve readability and professionalism.
The introduction lacks an explicit restatement of the research question in context; consider reiterating the RQ here to reinforce focus and guide the readerâs understanding.

The summary of The Silent Patient reads as plot narrative rather than analytical framing; condense descriptive content and integrate interpretive commentary that directly links to power dynamics in taboo relationships.
The phrase âthe second booksâ is imprecise; replace with a precise term (e.g. âthe second novelâ) to maintain academic clarity and register.
The discussion of Lolitaâs narrative remains largely descriptive; incorporate critical interrogation of how Humbertâs rhetoric manipulates reader sympathy to deepen analysis.
The Greek mythology section provides background but lacks explicit connection to your core argument; demonstrate how Freudâs theory directly informs the narrative techniques under review.
Repeating the research question verbatim in its own paragraph interrupts the flow; integrate it into a transitional sentence to maintain narrative coherence.
The methodology heading is clearly labelled and supports reader orientation within the essayâs structure.
This paragraph shifts back into summary rather than deepening your interpretive argument; focus on critical evaluation over plot recounting to strengthen analysis.
The observation of Humbertâs enjoyment in deceiving psychiatrists offers a nuanced insight into his manipulative narrator persona.
Methodology description lacks detail on source selection criteria and analytical procedures; expand on how you chose and evaluated your evidence to demonstrate methodological rigor.
Insightful close reading of Nabokovâs use of listing and imagery effectively links textual detail to underlying power dynamics.
This paragraph attempts multiple analytical directions without clear signposting; isolate distinct comparisons into separate sections to enhance coherence.
Effective synthesis of trauma parallels between Theo and Humbert strengthens the comparative argument and deepens insight into character motivations.
Extended direct quotation lacks subsequent analytical commentary; ensure each quote is followed by critical interpretation to link evidence to your argument.
Extended quotation of rationalizations around ânymphetsâ is presented without sufficient critical interrogation; evaluate the moral implications of this rhetoric to advance your argument.
Avoid addressing the âreaderâ directly in academic analysis; reframe observations in an objective third-person voice for scholarly tone.
Speculation about authorial intent (âNabokov may haveâ¦â) lacks supporting evidence; ground such claims in source-based arguments or remove them.
The transition into the Michaelides section is clearly signposted with an appropriate heading, aiding structural clarity.
Excellent analysis of Michaelidesâs rhetorical questions and disclaimers, showing how narrative voice manipulates reader trust.
The discussion of media misinterpretations and therapistâpatient boundaries deepens the critical evaluation of Theoâs unreliable narration.
The concluding quote from Theo is effectively used to illustrate his final act of rationalization and self-delusion.
A clear âComparative Analysisâ heading enhances structural coherence and signals the shift in focus.
This comparative discussion is diffuse and lengthy; consider breaking it into smaller thematic paragraphs to sharpen each point.
The paragraph ends abruptly and appears truncated; ensure complete arguments are presented and check for missing text due to formatting errors.
Clear evaluation of differing narrative perspectives (diary vs. therapistâs account) shows critical awareness of reliability layers.
In-text citations mix styles and lack consistent formatting (e.g., italics, parentheses); apply a uniform citation convention to enhance academic professionalism.
Bibliography entries display inconsistent formatting (varying use of italics, inconsistent DOI presentation); standardize the citation style (e.g., APA) across all references.
The Oudekerk et al. entry lacks consistent volume and issue formatting; verify that all references include complete journal information.
The entry for Nabokov lists the authorâs full name rather than surname first; adjust to âNabokov, V. V.â to conform with standard reference formatting.
The âTaboo Relationshipsâ heading clearly signals a new thematic focus and aids the essayâs structural flow.
The âConclusionâ heading is prominently placed, providing clear closure and guiding the reader through the final section.
The conclusion reiterates key findings but does not address methodological limitations or suggest avenues for further research; adding this would strengthen scholarly rigor.
Contrasting of narrator responses in this section effectively underscores thematic parallels and enhances reader insight.
The bibliography section is clearly titled and appropriately formatted as a distinct part of the report.
The inclusion of DOI links in references enhances traceability and meets best academic practice.
